other_observers: Andy Boyce; found by both of us
description: the bird was a sparrow sized bird, about the size or slightly larger than Vesper Sparrows seen before and after the observation. The overall color was brownish, with darker brown colored wings with two bold white wingbars. Below the bird was buff brownish colored. The tail pattern was seen very well, and was brown with two completey white outer tail feathers in a pattern very similar to a Vesper Sparrow.  The clincher in the ID was the call heard, which was a fairly long, dry rattle, with the last few notes dropping in pitch.Behaviors: The bird was flushed from the ground numerous times, and would fly a certain distance fairly low to the ground before landing again. We were unable to ever see the bird on the ground.Call: see belowPlumage: unknown
